author: Niche Gamer
text:
Resident Evil Requiem DLC may not arrive until after Code: Veronica Remake According to rumors, current rumors suggests Resident Evil Requiem’s planned story expansion DLC won’t launch until after the long-rumored Resident Evil Veronica remake, which is reportedly targeting a 2027 release window. Resident Evil Requiem launched earlier this year to strong reviews and sales, introducing players to FBI analyst Grace Ashcroft alongside series veteran Leon Kennedy. Capcom […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Toys for Bob says independence has allowed the studio to rediscover what it loves The studio behind Skylanders, Spyro Reignited Trilogy, and Crash Bandicoot 4 recently spoke about life after Activision, revealing that becoming independent has allowed the team to focus once again on the types of games it truly wants to create. According to studio leadership (via IGN), many developers felt disconnected from their strengths while working as […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Valor Mortis delayed as September release calendar becomes too crowded Just days after announcing a September 24 launch date during the Xbox Games Showcase, developer One More Level has confirmed that Valor Mortis is delayed. The Napoleonic soulslike game will now release on October 13 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC. The studio explained that the decision came after taking a closer look at […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
The Legend of Heroes: Trails from Zero and Trails to Azure launch for PS5 and Switch 2 in September NIS America has confirmed that The Legend of Heroes: Trails from Zero and The Legend of Heroes: Trails to Azure will launch on PlayStation 5 and Nintendo Switch 2 in September. Often referred to as the Crossbell duology, these two RPGs are considered some of the most beloved entries in Nihon Falcom’s long-running Trails series. […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Embroidered indie Scarlet Deer Inn releases in July Scarlett Deer Inn has build a world stitch-by-stitch with fully embroidered animations and hand-painted scenery. It's set to release in late July.

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Crimson Desert tops 6 million copies sold worldwide Pearl Abyss announced that Crimson Desert has officially surpassed 6 million copies sold worldwide just 83 days after launch. The open-world action-adventure game has been building momentum at an impressive rate since its release in March. After reaching 2 million sales within its first day, the title continued climbing steadily, hitting 3 million, 4 million, […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Wizardry 45th anniversary main visual revealed Not long after launching a big Wizardry 45th anniversary website, Drecom has revealed the main visual for their ongoing celebration of the series. The new visual was revealed (featured above) by illustrator Jun Suemi alongside a special survey for fans to share their nostalgic memories and messages for the series. Here’s a rundown on the new […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains launches this month Japanese developer BeXide has announced a release date for NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains, their new 3D puzzle game featuring voxel versions of Namco characters. NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains is set to launch across Windows PC (Steam), Switch, and Switch 2 later this month, on June 25th. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced game: Classic pixel art meets 3D […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Xbox posts new memo recapping CEO’s first 100 days, outlines “reset” for the company Xbox has published a memo from CEO Asha Sharma and executive VP and CCO Mat Booty outlining the CEO’s first 100 days, as well as a “reset” for the business. In the memo, the new CEO outlines her first 100 days at the company as well as the challenges Xbox has been facing, ranging from […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN dev explains why protagonist looks so young Instead of following Eve once again, Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN introduces players to Evie, a new protagonist tasked with hunting a dangerous terrorist organization known as Scarlet Sin. The action moves away from the ruined landscapes of the original game and into a massive cyberpunk city inspired by locations across Korea, Japan, and Hong Kong. […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Jason Momoa leaves Sony’s Helldivers movie Multiple reports indicate that Jason Momoa has exited Sony’s live-action Helldivers movie. New reports note Momoa is no longer attached to the film, though neither Sony nor the actor has publicly explained the reason behind the departure. Despite losing its headline star, the film remains in active development and the studio is reportedly already looking for […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Bradley the Badger gets new publisher Focus Entertainment Focus Entertainment has officially partnered with Day 4 Night Studios to publish Bradley the Badger, an upcoming action-adventure platformer from a team led by industry veterans Christian Cantamessa, known for his work on Red Dead Redemption, and Davide Soliani, the creative mind behind the Mario + Rabbids series. The game, which was revealed at The […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Waifu Wednesday: Evie from Stellar Blade BLOOD RAIN Welcome back to Waifu Wednesday, Niche Gamer’s column where we talk about characters, their appeal, and a little on their history. This week’s waifu is Evie from the upcoming game Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN. Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN is the upcoming sequel to 2024’s Stellar Blade from developer SHIFT UP. At the time, Stellar Blade made waves […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
[OPINION] PlayStation’s Japanese identity is slowly slipping away Have you noticed something strange over the past week? Because I certainly have. Kingdom Hearts IV showed up during a Nintendo Direct. Persona 6 was teased at the Xbox Games Showcase. The final chapter of the Final Fantasy VII Remake trilogy made its appearance at Summer Game Fest instead of a dedicated PlayStation event. Then […]
